sources.name: Google Trends
sources.method: manual export or browser-assisted public research

sources.name: YouTube public search/trending
sources.method: browser-assisted public research with rate limits
sources.allowedUse: format, hook, title, and topic benchmarking

sources.name: Reddit public communities
sources.method: public browsing, RSS where available, or official API if configured

sources.name: Reddit public JSON search
sources.method: public JSON endpoints with low rate, cache, and no account actions
sources.allowedUse: directional audience questions, objections, phrasing, recurring concerns

sources.name: Reddit subreddit rules manual review
sources.method: human/manual review of subreddit rules before any draft is approved
sources.allowedUse: community fit, promotion limits, post-format constraints

sources.name: X public search manual review
sources.method: human/manual public search review only; no login automation or account actions
sources.allowedUse: text hooks, timely topics, conversation style, thread formats

sources.name: X official API optional research
sources.method: official API only if user explicitly configures credentials; no engagement actions
sources.allowedUse: read-only trend/context research where permitted by plan and policy

sources.name: Meta Ad Library public review
sources.allowedUse: ad angle inspiration, compliance patterns, competitor offer framing

sources.name: Lu.ma public event pages
sources.method: user-provided public event URLs or manual research
sources.allowedUse: event positioning, title patterns, agenda/context checks

sources.name: Instagram public manual review
sources.method: human/manual or explicitly approved browser review only

sources.name: Facebook public manual review
sources.method: human/manual or explicitly approved browser review only
sources.allowedUse: page and group topic observation without automated collection

sources.name: User-provided documents and exports
sources.allowedUse: brand context, audience research, performance review
